stories.headline: A.J. Brown rides east to New England
stories.read: Well pardner, the Eagles just shipped their star receiver to the Patriots for a 2028 first and a 2027 fifth. Brown's a No. 1 target again — but on a greener offense, so don't go floodin' your ceiling too high. The real strike's back in Philly: every other pass-catcher just inherited a fatter cut of the throws. Go pan that creek before the saloon catches on.

stories.headline: Saquon goes under center — and called in Todd Gurley
stories.read: New Eagles OC Sean Mannion's haulin' in a McVay-style scheme, and Saquon says it'll be the most under-center, outside-zone work of his career. He even rang up Todd Gurley to learn the trade. Sirianni reckons he'll be "exceptional." Translation: still a bell-cow, still an RB1, and a healthy line means a bounce-back may be gushin' soon.

stories.headline: Christian Watson strikes gold: 4 years, $110.5M
stories.read: Green Bay just backed up the wagon for Watson — four years, $110.5M, a $31M signin' bonus, 15th among receivers at $27.6M a year. When an outfit pays a fella like that, they aim to feed him the ball. That's volume comin', pardner. Keep a close eye on his ADP — if the herd sleeps on him, there's your diamond in the rough.
